questions.question: What was the main aim of introducing the Permanent Settlement in Bengal?

options.A: To ensure a stable revenue for the British

questions.explanation: The British wanted a fixed and regular source of land revenue.

questions.question: Under the Permanent Settlement, who was responsible for collecting land revenue?
questions.explanation: Zamindars collected revenue from peasants and paid it to the British government.

questions.question: Why did many zamindars lose their lands under the Permanent Settlement?

options.A: They failed to pay revenue on time

questions.explanation: If zamindars failed to pay revenue, their lands were auctioned.

questions.question: Why did peasants often fall into debt during British rule?

options.A: High taxes and loans from moneylenders

questions.explanation: Heavy land revenue and loans pushed peasants into debt.

questions.question: What was the main use of indigo produced in India?
questions.explanation: Indigo dye was widely used in the textile industry in Europe.

questions.question: Why did European planters force peasants to grow indigo?

options.A: To earn profits from exporting dye

questions.explanation: Indigo was exported to Europe and brought large profits.

questions.question: What problem did peasants face when they accepted advances from planters?

options.A: They were forced to grow indigo

questions.explanation: Advances forced peasants to grow indigo instead of food crops.

questions.question: Why did peasants resist indigo cultivation?

options.A: It reduced soil fertility and food production

questions.explanation: Indigo exhausted the soil and reduced land for food crops.

questions.question: Which region in India was the centre of indigo cultivation?
questions.explanation: Bengal became the main centre of indigo cultivation.

questions.question: Which movement was started by peasants against indigo cultivation?
questions.explanation: Peasants protested against forced indigo cultivation.

questions.question: What role did moneylenders play in rural society?
questions.explanation: Moneylenders gave loans but charged high interest.

questions.question: Why did the British want a regular land revenue system?

options.A: To ensure stable income for administration

questions.explanation: Land revenue was the main financial source for the colonial government.

questions.question: What happened to many peasants who could not repay loans?
questions.explanation: Debt often resulted in peasants losing land.

questions.question: What type of crop is indigo?
questions.explanation: Cash crops are grown mainly for profit rather than consumption.

questions.question: Which group benefited most from indigo cultivation?
questions.explanation: Planters made large profits from exporting indigo.

questions.question: What action did peasants take during the Indigo Revolt?
questions.explanation: Peasants resisted by refusing to cultivate indigo.

questions.question: Which body was formed to investigate the Indigo Revolt?
questions.explanation: The British government set up the Indigo Commission.

questions.question: Why did peasants prefer growing food crops instead of indigo?

options.A: Food crops were essential for survival

questions.explanation: Peasants needed food crops for their livelihood.

questions.question: What was the result of heavy land revenue demands?
questions.explanation: Heavy taxes increased poverty and debt among peasants.

questions.question: What did British planters use to force peasants into indigo farming?
questions.explanation: Planters forced peasants by giving loans and applying pressure.

questions.question: Which system created a new class of landlords in Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Permanent Settlement recognised zamindars as landowners.

questions.question: What was the main reason for peasant protests in Bengal?
questions.explanation: Peasants protested against forced indigo farming.

questions.question: Why was indigo cultivation harmful to farmers?
questions.explanation: Indigo cultivation reduced soil fertility.

questions.question: Which factor led to peasant resistance against planters?
questions.explanation: Peasants resisted due to exploitation by planters.

questions.question: What does the Indigo Revolt show about colonial rural society?
questions.explanation: The revolt showed peasants resisting colonial exploitation.
